name: Milan Heat Vulnerability
description: Python pipeline that reproduces the Curio example 'Heterogeneous data + cross-grammar linked views' (docs/examples/09-heterogeneous-data-linked-views.md). Loads a Milan thermal raster + ERA5 weather + census polygons, computes per-pixel UTCI, aggregates it onto polygons, and projects the result for downstream AUTK_MAP / VIS_VEGA views.

kinds.label: Milan MRT Loader
kinds.description: Open the bundled 2022 Milan mean radiant temperature GeoTIFF and return the rasterio dataset for downstream zonal stats / UTCI computation.

kinds.label: ERA5 Milan Weather
kinds.description: Load the hourly ERA5 meteorological CSV (Td / Wind / RH per hour 'it') so a downstream UTCI step can pick the matching timestamp.

kinds.label: Milan Census Polygons
kinds.description: Load the Milan census GeoJSON (trimmed to the gt_65 column) as a GeoDataFrame so it can be reprojected and merged with UTCI.

kinds.label: UTCI from raster + weather
kinds.description: Compute the Universal Thermal Climate Index per raster pixel using pythermalcomfort. Reads raster + weather DataFrame via the Curio MERGE_FLOW pattern (arg[0] / arg[1]) and returns the UTCI grid + shape so the zonal-stats step can rebuild it.

kinds.label: UTCI Zonal Mean
kinds.description: Spatially join the UTCI grid into census polygons via rasterstats.zonal_stats, returning a GeoDataFrame keyed on geometry with mean UTCI + gt_65 columns. Inputs arrive in order (raster, UTCI tuple, polygons).

kinds.label: Reproject census to EPSG:3395
kinds.description: Reproject the merged census GeoDataFrame from UTM 32632 to EPSG:3395 (the projection AUTK_MAP's tile pipeline expects), drop polygons with non-positive UTCI, and tag the table with metadata.name='census' so downstream views can reference it by name.

kinds.label: Project gt_65 column
kinds.description: Project the (Geo)DataFrame down to just the gt_65 column — used to feed a boxplot view that summarises the older-adult-population distribution independently of UTCI.
